mapping.a_element: GPU-based parallel computation for accelerating numerical simulations (MD algorithms)
mapping.b_element: steepest descent analysis for solving stochastic partial differential equations (KPZ/SHE)

mapping.a_element: short-ranged and long-ranged interaction algorithms in MD
mapping.b_element: crossover distributions interpolating between Gaussian and Tracy-Widom (GUE) limits

mapping.a_element: performance speedup (flops/$) as a metric of computational efficiency
mapping.b_element: explicit formulas for marginal distributions as a metric of analytical tractability

brief_justification: Paper A focuses on hardware-accelerated numerical computation (GPU parallelization) for molecular dynamics, while Paper B employs analytical and asymptotic methods (steepest descent, weak asymmetry limits) to derive probability distributions for a stochastic PDE. The underlying mechanisms—parallelized simulation vs. rigorous mathematical analysis—are fundamentally distinct.
